message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Adding tip_weights + _select_tip_with_weights() to grounded_proposer.py for weighted tip selection

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task: remove temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor, add deterministic _cache_salt helper, thread cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signatures. Only file touched is grounded_proposer.py. What does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: We both must touch grounded_proposer.py (file-level split isn't possible here). My changes are localized to: __init__ (add tip_weights param) and the tip-selection block in propose_instruction_for_predictor (add _select_tip_with_weights() helper method + call site where set_tip_randomly picks a tip). I will NOT touch temperature modulation, cache_salt, or GenerateModuleInstruction/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signatures - that's all yours. Since edits are isolated per-agent sandboxes, no live clobbering risk, but confirm you'll avoid the tip-selection block and __init__ signature area (other than not colliding on the same lines) so our diffs merge cleanly if combined later. ACCEPT?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: We both must edit grounded_proposer.py and both touch propose_instruction_for_predictor (mine: remove temp modulation + add cache_salt; yours: tip_weights/_select_tip_with_weights). True disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: I'll go first since my change touches the top of propose_instruction_for_predictor (temp/epsilon removal) and threads cache_salt through several signature classes; my edits are additive/removal only around the temperature block and the input-field/forward signatures, NOT touching tip selection logic. I will avoid touching the tip-selection block (around rng.choice/TIPS) entirely. Once I send DONE, please implement your tip_weights changes on top of my version, being careful to preserve the cache_salt additions. Reply ACCEPT if OK, or propose the reverse order.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Accepted. I will only touch: propose_instruction_for_predictor's temperature/epsilon block (removing it) and cache_salt computation, plus generate_instruction_class/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule InputField additions, and GenerateModuleInstruction.forward call sites (adding cache_salt kwarg). I will not touch __init__ signature, tip_weights, or the tip-selection/rng.choice block. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: ACCEPT. I will only touch: __init__ signature (add tip_weights param + store it) and the tip-selection call site inside propose_instruction_for_predictor (where set_tip_randomly picks a tip from TIPS), plus add new helper method _select_tip_with_weights(). I will not touch temperature/epsilon logic, cache_salt, or the Signature classes. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Implemented tip_weights param + _select_tip_with_weights() helper, wired into the tip-selection block in propose_instructions_for_program. Did not touch temperature/epsilon logic, cache_salt, or Signature classes. Tests pass (tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py). Diff written to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished: removed temperature modulation/epsilon in propose_instruction_for_predictor, added _cache_salt(pred_i, demo_set_i) to GroundedProposer, added cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction signatures, threaded cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward and its 3 signature calls. Did not touch __init__, tip_weights, or the set_tip_randomly/rng.choice block - all yours.
